big_picture: Chief minister Mamata Banerjee said the arrest exposed the conspiracy behind the Malda violence, maintaining that AIMIM and the BJP are trying to create disturbances. The Rajasthan High Court has deleted its earlier critical remarks made on the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, which seeks to amend the Transgender Act 2019. The Rajya Sabha passed the Jan Vishwas Amendment Bill, with Piyush Goyal emphasising trust-based governance. The YSRCP opposed the single-capital model after the Andhra Bill became law, pushing a three-capital plan and ‘Plan B’. The Madhya Pradesh High Court quashed criminal proceedings against Railway Vigilance Officials for allegedly harassing a ticket examiner and subjecting him to caste-based slurs. The Rajya Sabha cleared the Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill, 2026, aiming to unify recruitment and service rules while strengthening command and governance across forces.

story_clusters.headline: Designed in backdrop of US tariffs, SEZ sops come into effect amid West Asia conflict
story_clusters.summary: Multiple sources report that India has implemented measures to address economic impacts linked to the West Asia conflict. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) extended the export credit period until June 30, 2026, while the government, led by PM Modi, reviewed fuel, fertilizer, and food supplies. Additionally, international bodies like the IMF, IEA, and World Bank are collaborating to manage the crisis's effects.

Legal.summary:
The Rajasthan High Court deleted its earlier critical remarks on the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026 and uploaded an updated version of the order.

The Madras High Court stayed a Tamil Nadu Government Order allowing police to exercise powers of the Special Executive Magistrate.

The Bombay High Court quashed a ₹7.5 lakh penalty on a farmer, ruling that clay residue alone is not sufficient to conclude illegal sand excavation.
Legal.top_article_title: Rajasthan High Court Deletes Earlier Remarks On Transgender Bill 2026, Uploads Updated Version

Civil Liberties.summary: The Parliament passed the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, which removes the right to self-determination for transgender persons. The Rajasthan High Court removed remarks from a March 30 judgment stating that trans rights must not be "rendered illusory by procedural constraints," calling them an "inadvertent inclusion." Jayna Kothari highlights in Transforming Rights that self-determination is crucial for improving employment and education outcomes for transgender people in India.
Civil Liberties.top_article_title: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026, and its Discontents

Governance.summary: The Department of Legal Affairs organized Swachhata Pakhwada in 2026 with active participation from officers and staff, promoting collective responsibility for cleanliness in workplaces and beyond. The IFSCA mandated a certification course for Key Managerial Personnel (KMPs) to enhance governance standards among intermediaries. IRDAI formed a Joint Expert Group to address implementation challenges related to Ind AS for insurers.
Governance.top_article_title: Swachhata Pakhwada, 2026 organised by Department of Legal Affairs with enthusiastic participation from officers and staff, reinforcing the spirit of collective responsibility towards maintaining cleanliness in workplaces and beyond

Politics.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) Bill with a voice vote, while Opposition members protested and staged a walkout. The Lok Sabha cleared a bill to make Amaravati the sole capital of Andhra Pradesh, with the YSR Congress Party staging a walkout. The Lok Sabha passed a bill declaring Amaravati as the capital of Andhra Pradesh, supported by the Telugu Desam Party, Bharatiya Janata Party, and Congress, while the YSR Congress Party opposed it.
Politics.top_article_title: Lok Sabha passes CAPF Bill with voice vote; Opposition members protest, walk out

Welfare.summary: The first article highlights that despite widespread support across party lines, the legislation for 33 per cent reservation for women in politics took 27 years to pass Parliament, becoming law in 2023. The second article notes the government's plan to reconvene Parliament for a special session to pass women's reservation legislation for 2029, with the NDA expressing confidence in cross-party support despite opposition concerns. The third article states that the Punjab Cabinet approved the 'Mukh Mantri Mawan Dheeyan Satkar Yojna', providing ₹1,000 monthly to women and ₹1,500 to Scheduled Castes women.
Welfare.top_article_title: Parties Yet To Walk The Talk On Women’s Reservation In Politics

Finance.summary:
GST applies to TDR, construction, and sale of units under Joint Development Agreements for residential projects intended for sale, with tax payable mainly on unsold units at completion to avoid double taxation.

The Reserve Bank of India issued a Master Direction under Section 35A of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, governing the facility for exchange of notes and coins for all banks.

The Reserve Bank of India tightened forex rules by banning non-deliverable rupee contracts.
Finance.top_article_title: GST on Joint Development Agreements for Residential Projects Intended for Sale

Energy.summary:
NTPC aims to reach 149 GW capacity by 2032 by prioritizing projects like the Singrauli Stage-III units and improving gas plant efficiency in Uttar Pradesh.

Europe is reconsidering nuclear energy as a solution to energy independence amid rising gas and fuel prices due to war.

The government has instructed refineries to redirect LPG feedstock to industries facing petrochemical shortages in sectors including packaging and condom manufacturing.
Energy.top_article_title: NTPC eyes 149 GW by 2032, expands renewable and nuclear footprint in UP: Diwakar Kaushik

Foreign.summary:
Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov will visit Delhi in mid-May for a BRICS meeting.

The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) notified India’s tax recovery pact with Japan, enabling cross-border tax collection assistance starting July 8, 2025.

The article argues that China views the U.S. Indo-Pacific Strategy as a constraint rather than a collapse of its regional influence.
Foreign.top_article_title: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov to visit Delhi mid May for BRICS meet

Health.summary: The mother of the RG Kar Medical College victim, who joined the BJP, will contest the West Bengal Assembly elections from Panihati, citing her fight for justice as the reason. The RG Kar case has become a political issue in Panihati, a TMC stronghold, nearly two years after the crime sparked nationwide outrage. India’s anti-obesity drug market is expanding with low-cost generics entering the market post-patent expiry, alongside new options in development.
Health.top_article_title: Bengal elections 2026: At Panihati, mother of RG Kar victim challenges TMC’s Tirthankar Ghosh

Labour.summary: The Surat textile hub is reducing operational days to five per week due to rising input costs linked to the West Asia conflict, according to Jitendra Vaktania, president of the South Gujarat Textile Processors Association. Workers across India are striking today in protest against the four new labour codes amid economic fallout from the US-Israel war on Iran. The All India Bank Employees' Association has requested Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman to order a CBI probe into Atanu Chakraborty’s resignation from HDFC Bank over ethical concerns.
Labour.top_article_title: West Asia conflict hits Surat textile hub hard as input costs rise; operational days cut down

Technology.summary: The Finance Ministry cleared over ₹1 lakh crore for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, with Cabinet approval expected by April-end. NCP Leader Nasiruddin Patwary stated that Sheikh Hasina surrendered too much space to India, and the BNP government appears to be moving in a similar direction regarding electoral engineering and constitutional reform in Bangladesh. The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (Meity) proposed IT rules tweaks to mandate platforms' compliance with advisories and standard operating procedures (SOPs).
Technology.top_article_title: Finance Ministry clears over ₹1 lakh cr for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0; Cabinet nod likely soon

Trade.summary: India agreed to extend the WTO e-commerce moratorium while opposing plurilateral agreements without safeguards, as stated by the Commerce Minister. President Trump signed an executive order imposing a 100% ad valorem duty on certain patented pharmaceuticals and ingredients. The CBIC notified revised tariff values for gold, silver, and edible oils under the Customs Act, effective from 03 April 2026.
Trade.top_article_title: India agrees to longer WTO e-commerce moratorium, opposes plurilateral push without safeguards

Infrastructure.summary:
The Reserve Bank of India now permits residents to exchange Indian Rupees at airport departure forex counters beyond immigration.

Prime Minister Narendra Modi will address a poll rally and hold a roadshow in Kerala as part of the NDA’s election campaign.

The National Highways Authority of India aims to reduce its debt to below ₹1.5 trillion in FY27 and become debt-free by 2030.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: RBI Allows Residents to Exchange INR at Airport Departure Forex Counters

Defence.summary:
A school strike in Iran’s Minab city on February 28, where a U.S. military strike allegedly hit the Shajareh Tayyebeh school, resulted in over 168 civilian deaths, testing compliance with International Humanitarian Law.

The Indian military is advised to procure anti-drone systems and integrate them with legacy weapon systems to create a layered defense umbrella against drone threats.

Defence Minister Rajnath Singh highlighted the Indian Navy’s critical role in securing 95% of India’s trade and energy supplies transported via sea routes.
Defence.top_article_title: School Strike in Iran: A Defining Test for International Humanitarian Law

Environment.summary:
53 gharials were released into the river at Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh as part of conservation efforts.

Governments remain divided over the timeline for publishing the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change’s AR7 report.

Disagreements between nations are affecting the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change’s latest assessment cycle.

Telecom.summary:
The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) released telecom subscription data for February 2026.

India's telecom subscriber base reached 1.32 billion in February, with the sector adding 7.31 million subscribers and a growth rate of 0.56%.

Nokia's report estimates India’s 5G subscriber base will exceed 1 billion by 2031, noting average monthly mobile data consumption per user rose to over 31 GB in 2025 from 27.5 GB in 2024.
Telecom.top_article_title: TRAI releases Telecom Subscription Data as on February 2026

Fintech.summary: In March, Unified Payments Interface transactions in India rose 11% month-over-month to 22.64 billion. Transaction volumes through the Unified Payments Interface reached 22.64 billion in March, up 24% from a year ago. The IMF highlighted both opportunities and risks of tokenization for emerging economies in its policy analysis.
Fintech.top_article_title: UPI Transactions Jump 11% MoM To 22.64 Bn In March

Agriculture.summary: A Stakeholders’ Meeting on the Climate Resilient Agriculture Programme was held on 30th March, 2026 at the ICAR Research Complex for Eastern Region in Patna with participation from scientists, project workers, and farmers from Gaya and Buxar districts of Bihar. India's dairy sector leaders in New Delhi emphasized the need for a climate-resilient ecosystem, focusing on integrated value chains, innovative financing, and scalable farmer-centric solutions. The Centre has intensified efforts to curb fertiliser black-marketing and reviewed Minimum Support Price (MSP) procurement.
Agriculture.top_article_title: Stakeholders Deliberate on Climate Resilient Agriculture at ICAR-RCER

DeepTech.summary:
Happiest Minds Technologies is preparing to launch an agentic service delivery platform as its first Agentic AI product for engineering, developed over 18 months and positioned as its key differentiator.

Sarvam AI, an India-based artificial intelligence startup, is nearing a funding round of $300 million to $350 million at a valuation of $1.5 billion to strengthen its position against competitors from the US and China.

Visa has introduced six AI tools for banks and merchants to address a 35% increase in credit card disputes since 2019, including a unified dispute management platform and pre-escalation interception capabilities.
DeepTech.top_article_title: Happiest Minds may launch its first Agentic AI for engineering soon

Education.summary: The Centre granted NCERT deemed university status, enabling it to independently offer degree programs to strengthen teacher education and research. The Centre granted NCERT deemed to be a varsity status, allowing it to independently offer degree programs to enhance teacher education and research. The APTF plans to stage protests on April 18 to demand the constitution of the 12th PRC from July 2023, a 30% interim relief, and the appointment of a headmaster and physical education teacher in every high school regardless of student strength.

Data Privacy.summary: The USTR stated that rules under India’s DPDP Act may impact the operations of US credit bureaus in India.
Data Privacy.top_article_title: DPDP Act rules may impact US credit bureaus' India operations: USTR

Urban.summary:
FY27 will introduce new real estate tax rules, simplifying some aspects while retaining others.

Maharashtra transferred 25 IAS officers, with Municipal Commissioner G. Shrikant moving from Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar to Navi Mumbai as Chief Executive Officer of the Jal Jeevan Mission.

The municipal body in Itanagar directed eateries to remove terms like ‘chicken’, ‘beef’, ‘pork’, and ‘mutton’ from their names, citing inappropriateness with public decency norms.
Urban.top_article_title: FY27 bring new real estate tax rules: What stays, what gets easier

articles.title: Income-tax Act, 2025 comes into force from 1st April, 2026
articles.summary: The government has replaced the six-decade-old Income-tax Act with the new Income-tax Act, 2025, which will come into force from April 1, 2026. The reform aims to simplify tax compliance by using clearer language and better structure. This is a major legislative change impacting India's tax framework.

articles.title: Comparative analysis of Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026
articles.summary: The Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026 proposes to increase eligibility limits for small companies, expanding their coverage and reducing compliance burdens. The reform aims to simplify regulatory requirements and promote ease of doing business in India. The bill is part of ongoing legislative discussions to modernize corporate governance. The analysis is provided by TaxGuru.

articles.title: Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026 A Comprehensive Analysis
articles.summary: The Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026 proposes converting certain criminal offenses under corporate law into civil penalties. This change aims to reduce prosecution risks for companies but increases monetary liabilities. The bill is under discussion and could impact corporate governance and compliance in India. The analysis is provided by TaxGuru.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerns criminal penalties in corporate law. The key takeaway is that offences are converted into civil penalties, reducing prosecution risk but increasing monetary liability. articles.title: Parties Yet To Walk The Talk On Women’s Reservation In Politics
articles.summary: The Women's Reservation Bill, first proposed in 1996 and widely supported across parties, was finally passed by Parliament in 2023 after 27 years. The law reserves 33% of seats in Parliament and state legislatures for women. Despite its passage, implementation remains pending, with parties yet to act on the legislation.
articles.raw_summary: First introduced in 1996 and widely supported across party lines, the legislation to ensure 33 per cent reservation for women in politics took 27 long years to be passed by Parliament, eventually becoming law in 2023.

articles.title: Why Bihar govt letter on Z-plus security for Nitish Kumar irks his JDU colleagues
articles.summary: A letter from the Bihar government granting Z-plus security to Chief Minister Nitish Kumar has sparked irritation among his colleagues in the Janata Dal (United) party. The decision has raised questions about administrative procedures and internal party dynamics. The controversy highlights tensions over security protocols and governance practices in the state.

articles.title: Jan Vishwas Bill Passes: Govt Decriminalises 1,000 Provisions To Ease Compliance
articles.summary: The Jan Vishwas Bill has been passed by the government, decriminalizing over 1,000 provisions to reduce litigation and improve ease of doing business. Union Minister Piyush Goyal pushed these reforms to simplify regulatory compliance. The move aims to boost economic activity by cutting bureaucratic hurdles and legal complexities.
articles.raw_summary: Piyush Goyal pushes reforms to cut litigation, boost ease of doing business

articles.title: 14 Landmark GST Rulings of March 2026
articles.summary: In March 2026, courts issued 14 landmark rulings on Goods and Services Tax (GST) proceedings, addressing the misuse of powers by authorities. The rulings enforced strict statutory limits and upheld taxpayer rights, ensuring fair and lawful tax administration. These decisions have direct implications for GST policy and enforcement in India. The article is sourced from TaxGuru.

articles.summary: The Punjab & Haryana High Court has held that the 1997 Service Rules mandating retiral benefits for employees of cooperative societies at par with government employees are ultra vires the parent statute and unenforceable. The Court observed that the State Government had no authority to further delegate its rule-making power to the Registrar, and such sub-delegation was neither expressly permitted nor implied under the governing Act. articles.title: Deduction under Section 80IAC of Income Tax Act for Startups
articles.summary: The Indian government has extended the eligibility period for startup tax exemptions under Section 80IAC of the Income Tax Act to March 31, 2030. To claim these benefits, startups must meet conditions set by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) and comply with regulatory requirements. This policy aims to support the growth of startups in India by providing tax relief during their early years.

articles.summary: The Union Ministry of Heavy Industries has approved a grant for setting up electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure in Kerala. The Kerala State Electricity Board (KSEB), the state-level nodal agency for the PM E-DRIVE scheme, filed the proposal. articles.title: Reassessment Notices Quashed Due to Approval by Wrong Authority Under Section 151
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) quashed reassessment notices issued by the tax department because they were approved by the wrong authority under Section 151 of the Income Tax Act. The court ruled that the Joint Commissioner of Income Tax (JCIT) lacked the jurisdiction to sanction reassessments beyond four years, making the notices legally unsustainable. articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether sanction by JCIT was valid for reassessment beyond four years. The court held that such approval was invalid, making the notices unsustainable. This decision impacts tax governance and underscores the importance of adhering to procedural requirements in tax administration.

articles.title: Land Rights Initiative turns 11!
Under Israel’s new law, Palestinians with terror convictions face mandatory death sentence
articles.summary: Israel's parliament, the Knesset, passed amendments to a law that mandates the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of terrorism. The changes eliminate the need for proper appeals, pardons, or judicial discretion. The amendments apply specifically to Palestinians with terror convictions and remove safeguards typically present in legal proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The Knesset cleared amendments allowing for executions without proper appeal, pardons or meaningful judicial discretion.

articles.title: Power to Grant Tax Exemption includes Power to Withdraw/Modify it in Public Interest; Beneficiary has No Vested Right: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the government has the power to withdraw or modify tax exemptions in the public interest, and beneficiaries do not have a legally enforceable right to such concessions. The court stated that the right to enjoy a concession is conditional and can be revoked. The ruling clarifies the government's authority over fiscal policies and tax exemptions.
articles.raw_summary: The recipient of a concession has no legally enforceable right against the Government to grant of a concession except to enjoy the benefits of the concession during the period of its grant. This right to enjoy is a defeasible one.  The post Power to Grant Tax Exemption includes Power to Withdraw/Modify it in Public Interest; Beneficiary has No Vested Right: Supreme Court appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: Entire Bank Deposits Can’t Be Taxed U/s 69A – Only Reasonable Commission Income Taxable
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that only commission income earned by a bank agent can be taxed as unexplained income under Section 69A, not the entire bank deposits. The tribunal upheld an 8% estimation of commission income as reasonable. This decision clarifies tax liability for commission agents and impacts how unexplained income is assessed under tax laws.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether full bank deposits of a commission agent can be taxed as unexplained income. The move aims to standardize regulatory knowledge, ensure compliance, and improve governance standards. This requirement applies to personnel in IFSCA-regulated entities, emphasizing the need for structured training to enhance operational and regulatory adherence.
articles.raw_summary: The circular addresses the need for standardized regulatory knowledge among intermediaries. It mandates certification for key personnel to ensure compliance and improve operational standards. The post IFSCA Mandates Certification Course for KMPs to Improve Governance in Intermediaries appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Faceless Assessment Mandated for SEZ to DTA Clearances for Concessional Duty Benefit
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has mandated a faceless assessment system for Bills of Entry filed by Special Economic Zone (SEZ) units when clearing goods to Domestic Tariff Area (DTA). This move aims to ensure uniformity and efficiency in customs clearance while maintaining post-assessment roles with jurisdictional officers. The circular applies to concessional duty benefits and is part of broader customs reform efforts.
articles.raw_summary: The circular mandates faceless assessment for Bills of Entry filed by SEZ units clearing goods to DTA. It ensures uniformity and efficiency while retaining post-assessment roles with jurisdictional officers. The post Faceless Assessment Mandated for SEZ to DTA Clearances for Concessional Duty Benefit appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Penalty Imposed for Failure to Appoint Independent Director from IICA Databank
articles.summary: A company and its officer were penalized for failing to appoint an independent director from the IICA databank as required by Section 150(1) of the law. This highlights the legal obligation for companies to comply with corporate governance norms.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved non-compliance with Section 150(1) due to improper appointment of an independent director. articles.title: RP Suspended for Accepting Ineligible Resolution Plan in Violation of CIRP Regulations
articles.summary: A regulatory authority suspended a Resolution Professional (RP) for three years after finding that they accepted an ineligible resolution plan from a non-listed applicant, violating Regulation 39 of the CIRP (Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process) regulations. The breach led to disciplinary action, highlighting strict compliance requirements under insolvency laws. This case underscores the importance of adherence to regulatory norms in corporate insolvency proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The authority held that accepting a plan from a non-listed applicant violated Regulation 39. articles.title: Government confident of cross-party support for women’s quota bills
articles.summary: The government plans to reconvene Parliament for a special session to pass bills reserving seats for women in legislatures by 2029. The ruling NDA coalition expects cross-party support despite opposition concerns about timing and motives. The move reflects growing political focus on women's voting influence and past broad support for such legislation.
articles.raw_summary: The government plans to reconvene Parliament for a special session to pass women's reservation legislation for 2029. Despite opposition parties questioning the timing and motives, they've largely supported the proposal itself. The NDA is confident due to broad past support and women's growing influence as voters, anticipating passage of key bills.

articles.title: Section 80P Deduction Allowed on Bank Interest – ITAT Follows Tumkur Merchants, Distinguishes Totagars
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that interest earned on bank deposits made from surplus business funds is considered business income, not income from other sources. This allows businesses to claim tax deductions under Section 80P. The ruling follows a previous decision in Tumkur Merchants' case and distinguishes it from the Totagars case. The decision impacts how businesses classify and tax their interest income.
articles.raw_summary: The ruling holds that deposits made from surplus business funds are part of operational activity. Interest earned on such deposits is business income and not income from other sources. Therefore, Section 80P benefit was granted. The post Section 80P Deduction Allowed on Bank Interest   ITAT Follows Tumkur Merchants, Distinguishes Totagars appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Bangladesh’s reform plan is trapped in a legal design of its own making
articles.summary: Bangladesh's attempt to reform its legal and policy frameworks is facing obstacles due to the very legal architecture it created. The debate centers on whether the system can handle selective changes without collapsing. The article highlights the tension between reform goals and the rigidity of existing laws.
articles.raw_summary: The core debate is not only about whether reform should proceed, but whether the legal architecture built to deliver it can withstand selective change.

articles.title: Karnataka ends road tax exemption for electric cars: What it means for buyers
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has decided to end the road tax exemption for electric cars and introduce a lifetime road tax ranging from 5% to 10% based on the vehicle's price. This change, part of a new amendment bill, will make electric vehicles more expensive for buyers in the state. The policy aims to standardize taxation for EVs while generating revenue for the government.
articles.raw_summary: Electric vehicles in Karnataka will become costlier as the government plans to end road tax exemption and introduce lifetime tax of 5% to 10% based on vehicle price under a new amendment bill.

articles.title: Vague Penalty Notice Invalid: ITAT Deletes 200% Penalty Under Section 270A
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a penalty notice under Section 270A of the Income Tax Act was invalid because it failed to specify whether the taxpayer had underreported or misreported income. The tribunal emphasized that proper identification of the charge is mandatory for valid penalty proceedings. As a result, a 200% penalty imposed on the taxpayer was deleted. The ruling highlights the importance of clear and specific notices in tax penalty cases.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that failure to specify underreporting or misreporting in notice invalidates penalty. Proper identification of charge is mandatory for valid proceedings. The post Vague Penalty Notice Invalid: ITAT Deletes 200% Penalty Under Section 270A appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Redeployment of Withdrawn Cash Cannot Trigger Section 68 Addition: Source Proven
articles.summary: An Income Tax Tribunal ruled that cash deposits backed by prior withdrawals cannot be taxed as unexplained income under Section 68 if there is no evidence of alternative use. The ruling clarifies that the source of such deposits is considered proven unless proven otherwise. The decision impacts tax assessment practices and provides legal clarity on cash transaction disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal examined whether cash deposits backed by prior withdrawals can be taxed as unexplained income. It ruled that in absence of evidence showing alternative use of cash, the source stands explained. The post Redeployment of Withdrawn Cash Cannot Trigger Section 68 Addition: Source Proven appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: NGT Flags Non-Compliance With Directions For Protection Of Wetlands In Tikamgarh; Directs Formation Of Task Force For Effective Protection
articles.summary: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has criticized authorities in Tikamgarh for failing to protect wetlands despite earlier orders. The tribunal noted no serious efforts were made to remove encroachments, stop untreated water discharge, or safeguard wetland areas under the Wetland Rules, 2017. Justice Pushpa Sathyanarayana and Expert Member Sudhir directed the formation of a task force to ensure effective protection of these wetlands.
articles.raw_summary: The National Green Tribunal has expressed displeasure at the failure of authorities to comply with its earlier directions for the protection and conservation of wetlands in Tikamgarh. The Tribunal observed that no sincere efforts had been made by the concerned authorities to implement the Wetland Rules, 2017, particularly in relation to the removal of encroachments, prevention of discharge of untreated water, and protection of wetland areas. articles.title: Fresh Claim of Section 54F Cannot Be Rejected Mechanically – ITAT Remands for Reconsideration
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that claims under Section 54F of the Income Tax Act cannot be rejected mechanically if supported by facts, even if not initially made in the return. The case was remanded for reconsideration, emphasizing that such claims should be examined based on merit. This ruling impacts how tax deductions are processed and could influence future tax policy interpretations. The decision underscores the importance of factual substantiation in tax claims.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was denial of deduction under Section 54F as a fresh claim not made in return. The ruling remanded the matter, holding such claims can be examined if supported by facts. articles.title: Supreme Court Monthly Digest: February 2026
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Department of Telecommunications (DoT) cannot levy interest for delays caused by its own inaction. The Court held that interest is only payable from the expiry of the notice period (December 8, 2014), not from an earlier order date, as the delay was solely attributable to the DoT. This decision impacts government authorities' liability for administrative delays in regulatory matters.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that cash deposits during demonetisation linked to documented business sales cannot be taxed as unexplained income under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act unless there is proof of bogus transactions. This decision provides clarity on tax treatment of demonetisation-era cash deposits for businesses.

articles.title: Supreme Court Weekly Digest February 21 - 28, 2026
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Department of Telecommunications (DoT) cannot levy interest for delays caused by its own inaction. The Court upheld a decision by the Telecom Disputes Settlement and Appellate Tribunal (TDSAT), stating interest is only payable from the expiry of the notice period (December 8, 2014), not from an earlier court order in 2013. The delay in issuing the demand was solely attributable to the DoT's failure to act promptly.

The collaboration aims to leverage waste treatment and biofuel production, aligning with India's broader push for cleaner energy alternatives. The deal reflects growing private sector interest in renewable energy, though it does not directly announce new government policies.

articles.title: CBIC Notifies Revised Tariff Values for Gold, Silver & Edible Oils wef 03 April 2026
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has notified revised tariff values for gold, silver, and edible oils, effective from April 3, 2026. The changes update customs valuation tables under the Customs Act while maintaining existing rates for key commodities. This ensures continuity in import duties for these goods. The notification was issued by CBIC, a regulatory body under the Ministry of Finance.
articles.raw_summary: CBIC updated tariff value tables under the Customs Act while retaining existing rates for key commodities. The ruling ensures continuity in customs valuation for imports like gold, silver, and edible oils. articles.title: Teachers’ federation plans to stage protests on April 18
articles.summary: The All India Primary Teachers' Federation (APTF) has announced plans to stage protests on April 18, demanding the government constitute the 12th Pay Review Commission (PRC) from July 2023. They are also urging a 30% interim relief for teachers and the appointment of a Head Master (HM) and Physical Education Teacher (PET) in every high school, regardless of student strength. The federation's demands highlight issues related to education policy and workforce welfare.
articles.raw_summary: APTF urges the government to constitute the 12th PRC with effect from July 2023, announce 30% interim relief and appoint a HM and a PET in every high school, irrespective of student strength

articles.title: NTPC unit signs green ammonia supply pact with SECI
articles.summary: NTPC's renewable energy unit has signed an agreement with SECI to supply 70,000 metric tonnes of green ammonia annually to Krishna Phoschem Limited. SECI will act as the intermediary procurer. This deal supports India's push for cleaner energy sources and aligns with global decarbonization efforts.
articles.raw_summary: The agreement involves the supply of 70,000 metric tonnes per annum of green ammonia to Krishna Phoschem Limited with SECI as an intermediary procurer.

articles.title: New creditor-led IBC framework to put in place safeguards for stressed firm's management
articles.summary: The government is introducing new insolvency rules under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) to strengthen the creditor-led resolution process for stressed companies. These rules will impose strict financial transaction limits on distressed firms, requiring approval from the committee of creditors. The management of these companies will continue operating under the supervision of a resolution professional, with enhanced oversight and reporting requirements to ensure transparency and protect
articles.raw_summary: New creditor-led insolvency rules will impose strict financial transaction limits on stressed companies, requiring committee of creditors' approval. This aims to safeguard the rescue process and prevent misconduct. Management will continue operations under a resolution professional's supervision, with enhanced oversight and reporting obligations to ensure transparency and protect creditor interests.

articles.title: GST Applicable on Capital Contribution of Leasehold Property Due to Supply of Service: AAR Odisha
articles.summary: The Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) Odisha ruled that contributing leasehold land and constructed property to a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) is not a sale of immovable property but constitutes a supply of service. The decision is based on the consideration being in the form of profit-sharing rights. This ruling clarifies the tax treatment under GST for such transactions, impacting businesses and tax policies.
articles.raw_summary: The Authority held that contribution of leasehold land and constructed property to an LLP is not a sale of immovable property. It constitutes a supply of service as consideration exists in the form of profit-sharing rights. The post GST Applicable on Capital Contribution of Leasehold Property Due to Supply of Service: AAR Odisha appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Ex-Parte Succession Certificate Set Aside – SC Protects Minor’s Rights, Allows Fresh Hearing
articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside an ex parte succession certificate granted without hearing a minor heir, ruling that denial of hearing vitiates legal proceedings. The case involved protecting the rights of a minor in inheritance matters. The court allowed a fresh hearing, emphasizing the importance of fair process in succession disputes. This ruling reinforces protections for vulnerable parties in legal proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether a succession certificate granted without impleading a minor heir was valid. The Supreme Court set aside the ex parte order, holding denial of hearing to a minor vitiates proceedings. The post Ex-Parte Succession Certificate Set Aside   SC Protects Minor’s Rights, Allows Fresh Hearing appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Government moves to end toll waiver on ID display; no cash at booths from April 10
articles.summary: The Indian government will enforce cashless toll payments starting April 10, eliminating cash transactions at toll plazas. Vehicles without FASTags must pay 1.25 times the normal fee via UPI or face penalties. Exempted individuals must now use special FASTags or annual passes to prevent misuse, aiming to reduce disputes and enable signal-free tolling.
articles.raw_summary: Toll payments will go cashless from April 10, with no cash transactions accepted. Vehicles without FASTags can pay via UPI at 1.25 times the normal fee, or face penalties. Exempted individuals must now use special FASTags or annual passes to prevent misuse and reduce arguments at toll plazas, paving the way for signal-free tolling.

articles.title: Budget session to be extended? Parliament may reconvene in 3rd week of April to raise Lok Sabha seats to 816: Report
articles.summary: The government is planning to extend the session by 2 to 3 days in the third week of April to bring a bill to increase the number of Lok Sabha seats to 816, the agencies said. The idea is to have at least 273 seats in Lok Sabha (33 per cent) reserved for women, news agency PTI said, quoting sources.
articles.raw_summary: The government is planning to extend the session by 2 to 3 days in the third week of April to bring a bill to increase the number of Lok Sabha seats to 816, the agencies said. articles.title: No Tax on Property Deals Within 10% Gap: ITAT Allows Safe Harbour on DVO Value
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a 10% safe harbour applies to property valuations by the District Valuation Officer (DVO), not just stamp duty values. This means minor valuation differences within 10% cannot trigger tax additions under section 56(2)(x) of the Income Tax Act. The decision provides clarity on tax treatment for property transactions where valuations are close but not identical.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal held that the 10% safe harbour applies even to DVO valuations, not just stamp duty values. Minor valuation differences cannot trigger tax additions under section 56(2)(x). The post No Tax on Property Deals Within 10% Gap: ITAT Allows Safe Harbour on DVO Value appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Could end of soy pact lead to Amazon deforestation surge?
articles.summary: Brazil's soy industry agreement, aimed at reducing deforestation, has collapsed, raising concerns among experts about a possible surge in Amazon deforestation. The agreement was a key environmental policy tool to curb forest loss linked to soy farming. Its failure could undermine trust in Brazil's soy market and accelerate forest destruction. The article highlights the environmental stakes of policy decisions in global agriculture.
articles.raw_summary: The collapse of one of Brazil’s most successful environmental agreements could knock trust in its soy market and impact forests, experts warn The post Could end of soy pact lead to Amazon deforestation surge? appeared first on Dialogue Earth .

articles.summary: The Madras High Court restored a taxpayer's GST registration after it was canceled, citing limitation grounds. The court allowed restoration subject to compliance conditions, such as filing returns and paying dues. This ruling follows consistent judicial precedents on GST-related appeals. The decision impacts businesses facing GST cancellation due to procedural lapses.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court held a special sitting to modify its previous order allowing certain candidates to appear for the Rajasthan Sub Inspector Police/Platoon Commander Recruitment Examination 2025, scheduled for April 5. The Court restricted the benefit to only the petitioner, Suraj Mal Meena, reversing its earlier decision that extended the opportunity to similarly placed candidates. The case pertains to a 2021 recruitment notification for Sub Inspector positions in Rajasthan.

articles.summary: The Customs Authority for Advance Rulings (CAAR) permitted the withdrawal of a classification dispute concerning aluminum hollow profiles. The decision was granted because no ruling had been issued at the time of the request. This confirms that applicants can withdraw such applications before a final decision under Regulation 20. The ruling applies to a trade/tax classification matter under Indian customs regulations.

articles.title: Govt to forego ₹1,800 cr in revenue after ending petchem import tax, source says
articles.summary: The Indian government has decided to waive import taxes on 40 petrochemical products, leading to a revenue loss of ₹1,800 crore. The exemption is valid until June 30. The move aims to boost domestic industry competitiveness by reducing input costs for petrochemical manufacturers. The decision was reportedly made by the government, as cited by a source.

articles.title: Supreme Court upholds harsher punishment for higher authority
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld harsher punishments for higher-ranking officials accused of service misdeeds, citing their increased responsibility. The apex court reinstated the dismissal of a senior bank manager, forced retirement of an officer, and salary cut for a gunman involved in fund misappropriation. The ruling emphasized that courts should exercise restraint in interfering with disciplinary actions by authorities.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court ruled that higher rank officials face harsher penalties for service misdeeds due to increased responsibility. Upholding differential punishments for bank employees involved in misappropriating funds, the apex court reinstated a senior manager's sacking, while an officer faced forced retirement and a gunman a salary cut. Courts must exercise restraint in interfering with disciplinary actions.

articles.title: Changes to trans rights act will worsen access to healthcare for vulnerable individuals
articles.summary: The article reports that proposed changes to India's trans rights act could worsen healthcare access for vulnerable individuals by giving government and medical authorities more control over identity determination. This may discourage some groups from relying on institutions that are already discriminatory. The changes could have significant implications for transgender rights and healthcare equity.
articles.raw_summary: Government and medical authorities determining identity, will discourage some groups from relying on institutions that are discriminatory.

articles.title: Supreme Court orders NIA/CBI probe, central forces in West Bengal’s Malda after attack on judicial officials
articles.summary: The Supreme Court criticized the West Bengal government over a mob attack on judicial officers in Malda and ordered the deployment of central forces. Chief Justice of India D.Y. Chandrachud directed an independent investigation by the CBI or NIA into the incident. The court's intervention highlights concerns over law and order and judicial safety in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court slammed West Bengal over Malda mob attack on judicial officers. CJI Kant ordered central forces deployment, independent CBI or NIA probe.

articles.title: Tender reforms introduced in infrastructure projects: PWD Minister
articles.summary: The Delhi government has reformed its tendering process. Earnest Money Deposit submissions are now entirely online for Public Works Department and Flood Control   Irrigation Department projects. This digital shift eliminates physical deposits, reducing human interface and potential bias. The move aims to ensure fair opportunities for honest contractors and enhance accountability in public fund utilization.

articles.summary: A Supreme Court-appointed panel, the Central Empowered Committee, reported that Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan violated a 2000 Supreme Court order by issuing de-reservation orders, leading to rampant illegal sand mining in the Chambal region. articles.summary: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) has accused social media users of illegally accessing and circulating its official documents without authorization. The case highlights concerns over unauthorized dissemination of sensitive government information and potential violations of data security protocols.

articles.title: Company Cannot Give Loan To Director Without Special Resolution In General Meeting : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a company cannot lend money to its director unless a special resolution is passed in a general meeting. The loan must also be directly related to the company's principal business. This decision clarifies legal obligations under corporate governance rules in India.
articles.raw_summary: The loan advanced to the director from company's funds must relate to the principal business of the company.

articles.title: Agricultural Land Capital Gain Addition Deleted in absence of development or trading intent
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that gains from the sale of agricultural land are not taxable if there is no intent to develop or trade the land. The tribunal held that the land met conditions under Section 2(14)(iii) of the Income Tax Act, making the capital gains non-taxable. The case clarified that such sales do not constitute business income without development or trading intent.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved denial of exemption on sale of agricultural land. The Tribunal held that the land met conditions of Section 2(14)(iii), making gains non-taxable. The case examined whether land sale constituted business income. The Tribunal ruled that absence of development or trading intent invalidated such classification. The post Agricultural Land Capital Gain Addition Deleted in absence of development or trading intent appeared first on TaxGuru .

The plan includes Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes, controls on fuel prices, and a proposed shipping risk pool to stabilize the economy. The measures aim to mitigate economic disruptions caused by the regional instability.

articles.title: AO Cannot Apply 50% Presumptive Tax Rate Without Proof of Professional Income
articles.summary: An income tax tribunal ruled that tax authorities cannot apply a 50% presumptive tax rate under section 44ADA without proof of professional income. The ruling clarifies that business income under section 44AD must be taxed separately and strictly as per law. The decision impacts how tax officers assess professional income under presumptive tax schemes. The ruling was issued by a tribunal and has immediate legal implications for tax assessments.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal ruled that business income under section 44AD cannot be taxed using section 44ADA provisions. Presumptive schemes must be applied strictly as per law. The post AO Cannot Apply 50% Presumptive Tax Rate Without Proof of Professional Income appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Client Liable for CA Fees: Bombay HC Confirms Cheque Bounce Case After 9 Years
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court upheld a conviction in a cheque bounce case after nine years, confirming that the accused could not disprove liability under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act. The court found the complainant's evidence sufficient and the presumption of liability unrebutted. The case involved a dispute over fees charged by a chartered accountant, with the court ruling in favor of the complainant.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether the accused could disprove liability under Section 138. The court upheld conviction as the presumption remained unrebutted and evidence supported the complainant’s claim. articles.title: Crushing & Sizing of Limestone Not Manufacture as No New Product Emerges: AAR Odisha
articles.summary: The Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) in Odisha ruled that crushing and sizing of limestone does not qualify as 'manufacture' for tax purposes since no new product with distinct characteristics is created. The mineral remains unchanged, and the activity is considered processing rather than manufacturing. This decision impacts tax liability and regulatory classification for businesses in the limestone sector.
articles.raw_summary: The Authority held that processing activities like crushing and sizing do not create a new product with distinct characteristics. Since the mineral remains unchanged, the activity does not qualify as manufacture. The post Crushing   Sizing of Limestone Not Manufacture as No New Product Emerges: AAR Odisha appeared first on TaxGuru .

The withdrawal occurred because further testing of the fuel was needed, so no GST classification or rate ruling was issued. This case underscores that tax authorities do not provide rulings when applications are withdrawn before completion.
articles.raw_summary: The applicant withdrew its advance ruling request citing the need for further testing of its bio-diesel product. The Authority disposed of the application without addressing GST classification or rates. The case highlights that no ruling is issued when applications are withdrawn. articles.summary: NTPC, India's state-owned power utility, is prioritizing the execution of key energy projects in Uttar Pradesh, including the commissioning of Singrauli Stage-III units, improving gas availability for Dadri and Auraiya plants, and accelerating solar projects. The company aims to expand its total capacity to 149 GW by 2032, focusing on renewable and nuclear energy. These efforts support India's broader energy transition and infrastructure development goals.

These include the timely commissioning of the Singrauli Stage-III units, improving gas availability for the Dadri and Auraiya gas-based plants to enhance their Plant Load Factor (PLF), and ensuring the on-schedule execution of solar projects across Uttar Pradesh.

This action aims to curb speculative trading and support the Indian rupee against the US dollar.

articles.title: 11-Year Delay Not Condoned: Mere Rectification Plea No Excuse
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) dismissed an appeal due to an unexplained delay of over 11 years, stating that mere rectification pleas are insufficient to condone such delays. The tribunal ruled that no valid reasons were provided to justify the delay, emphasizing strict adherence to procedural timelines. This decision underscores the importance of timely compliance in tax appeals and may influence future tax policy enforcement.

He argued the move, aimed at raising Rs 250 crore, is fiscally shortsighted and contradicts global clean mobility incentives.

articles.summary: A consumer commission in Thane has directed Ola Electric to replace a faulty scooter or refund the consumer with interest, along with compensation for mental agony and costs. The ruling highlights deficiencies in Ola Electric's service and raises concerns about consumer rights in the electric vehicle sector. The order follows a complaint about a defective scooter and poor after-sales support.

articles.title: Karnataka withdraws exemption, to slap lifetime tax on EVs, except two-wheelers
articles.summary: Karnataka has withdrawn lifetime road tax exemptions for electric cars, jeeps, buses, and private vehicles, except for two-wheelers. The move aims to generate an additional Rs 250 crore in revenue, with tax rates varying based on vehicle price. This decision has drawn criticism as a regressive step against EV adoption.

articles.title: Basmati exporters seek relief as shipping lines levy charges amid disruption due to war
articles.summary: Basmati rice exporters, represented by an industry body, are seeking relief from shipping lines that have imposed charges for delays. The exporters argue that these charges are unjustified, as delays stem from operational decisions or geopolitical factors like the war. The dispute highlights potential regulatory gaps in trade logistics and could prompt government or industry intervention to address the issue.
articles.raw_summary: The industry body contends that shipping lines cannot legally impose charges for delays resulting from their own operational decisions or external geopolitical developments

articles.title: Real GDP growth may slow on costly crude: Credit rating agencies
articles.summary: Credit rating agencies Icra, Care Ratings, and Crisil have projected India's GDP growth at around 6.5% in FY27, citing high crude oil prices as a key factor. Icra assumes an average crude price above $85 per barrel, while Care Ratings uses $100 per barrel. Crisil notes that strong corporate balance sheets may mitigate credit risks despite these challenges.
articles.raw_summary: Icra projected GDP growth at 6.5% in FY27, assuming average crude price remains in excess of $85 a barrel this year, while Care Ratings projected a similar growth on higher average fuel-price assumptions-at $100 per barrel. However, Crisil expects robust balance sheet structures to provide sufficient undergirding against credit risks for an overwhelming percentage of industries it covers.

articles.title: The Third Islamic Republic
articles.summary: The article examines the unintended consequences of a war involving Iran, analyzing its impact on the Middle East and the broader global order. It explores how regional conflicts could reshape geopolitical dynamics, affecting India's foreign policy, energy security, and strategic interests in West Asia. The analysis highlights potential shifts in alliances, economic disruptions, and security challenges that may arise from prolonged instability in the region.
articles.raw_summary: A war’s unintended consequences—for Iran, the Middle East, and the global order.

articles.title: Telangana passes Bill that cuts salary if children neglect parents
articles.summary: The Telangana Assembly passed a bill that allows salary deductions for government employees who fail to support their elderly parents. This is the first such law in India targeting employee accountability for elder care.

articles.title: Bihar hooch tragedy: 5 killed, 12 arrested; Opposition slam NDA govt, probe underway
articles.summary: Five people died in East Champaran, Bihar, after consuming suspected illicit liquor. The local police station head (SHO) has been suspended, and a Special Investigation Team (SIT) along with an Anti-Liquor Task Force (ALTF) is probing the incident. Opposition leaders have criticized the NDA government for weak enforcement of liquor bans, demanding stricter action.
articles.raw_summary: Five die after suspected hooch in East Champaran Bihar. SIT and ALTF probe is underway as the SHO has been suspended. Opposition leaders slam liquor ban enforcement.

articles.title: Premium petrol and diesel prices rise amid crude surge: What it means for consumers
articles.summary: Premium petrol and diesel prices in India have risen from April 1, with XP100 petrol and premium diesel becoming costlier. Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) rates were revised after a sharp spike, and commercial LPG prices have increased by over ₹300 since March. The surge in crude oil prices is the primary driver of these changes, which will impact consumer costs and potentially trigger government responses like subsidies or price controls.
articles.raw_summary: Premium fuel prices have increased from April 1, with XP100 petrol and premium diesel becoming costlier. ATF rates were revised after a sharp spike, while commercial LPG prices have risen by over ₹300 since March.

articles.title: Informal sector steps up coal stocking over price concerns
articles.summary: Energy-intensive units in India's informal sector are increasing coal stockpiles due to rising costs of imported coal, despite limited storage capacity. This trend suggests market expectations of further price hikes. The situation highlights potential vulnerabilities in India's energy supply chain and may prompt policy responses to stabilize prices or boost domestic production.
articles.raw_summary: While domestic production remains stable, imported coal costs have increased, prompting these energy-intensive units to build reserves despite limited storage capacity. This trend reflects market expectations of further price hikes.

articles.title: CBSE 2026–27: 3-Language Formula From Class 6; 2-Level Maths, Science in Class 9
articles.summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has announced a new policy for the 2026-27 academic year, mandating a 3-language formula from Class 6 and introducing two levels of Maths and Science in Class 9. The changes aim to standardize language and subject offerings across schools affiliated with CBSE, impacting millions of students nationwide. The policy is set to be implemented from the next academic session.
articles.raw_summary: CBSE 2026–27: 3-Language Formula From Class 6; 2-Level Maths, Science in Class 9

articles.title: WTO talks end in deadlock after Brazil blocks deal on e-commerce duties
articles.summary: Talks at the World Trade Organization (WTO) ended in a deadlock after Brazil blocked an extension of a moratorium on e-commerce duties backed by the US. WTO Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala warned that countries may impose new digital duties if no agreement is reached. The dispute has shifted to Geneva for further negotiations, raising concerns about the future of global digital trade rules.
articles.raw_summary: Brazil blocks US backed WTO e commerce moratorium extension in Yaounde deadlock, as Ngozi Okonjo Iweala warns of new digital duties and reform talks shift to Geneva.

articles.title: Right To Be Considered For Promotion Is A Fundamental Right: P&H HC
articles.summary: The Punjab & Haryana High Court ruled that the right to be considered for promotion is a fundamental right for government employees. The court found authorities had wrongly denied promotions by misreading service rules and granted notional promotions with back benefits. The ruling underscores the need for accurate interpretation of service rules in promotion matters. This decision impacts government employees' career progression and service conditions.
articles.raw_summary: The Court found that authorities wrongly denied promotion by misreading applicable service rules. It granted notional promotion with consequential benefits. articles.title: No GST Profiteering Found as ITC Benefit Declined Post-GST: GSTAT
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) ruled that no profiteering occurred under GST because the Input Tax Credit (ITC) benefit declined after GST implementation. The tribunal held that no additional benefit arose, meaning businesses were not obligated to pass on ITC benefits to buyers. This decision impacts how GST profiteering cases are assessed and could influence future regulatory actions.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that no profiteering occurred as the ITC-to-purchase value ratio declined after GST implementation. It ruled that no additional benefit arose, eliminating the obligation to pass on ITC benefits to buyers. The post No GST Profiteering Found as ITC Benefit Declined Post-GST: GSTAT appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Securing the Skies: MoD’s drone vulnerability framework marks shift to secure warfare systems
articles.summary: The Ministry of Defence (MoD) has introduced a new framework to secure drone procurement by mandating vulnerability testing, secure firmware protocols, and supply chain audits. This initiative aims to shift defence systems toward cyber-resilient, secure-by-design standards. The move addresses growing concerns over drone vulnerabilities in military operations. The framework is part of broader efforts to enhance India's defence preparedness against emerging cyber threats.
articles.raw_summary: The new MoD framework transforms drone procurement by integrating vulnerability testing, secure firmware protocols, and supply chain audits—marking a shift toward cyber-resilient, secure-by-design defence systems.

articles.title: Petrol shock, free rides & more: How is Pakistan dealing with Hormuz energy crisis
articles.summary: Pakistan is facing a severe energy crisis with soaring fuel prices, leading the government to introduce free public transport in Islamabad and Punjab for 30 days to ease the burden. Despite a revised petrol price, public anger persists, and similar measures are being considered in Karachi and Bangladesh. The crisis is driven by global energy disruptions and pressure from the IMF, highlighting regional economic challenges.

The government aims to ease economic burdens amid global energy disruptions and IMF pressure, with similar measures seen in Karachi and Bangladesh.

articles.summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has announced new curriculum reforms, including a three-language formula starting from Class 6 by 2026 and a two-level structure for mathematics and science in Class 9. The reforms are set to be implemented in phases, with full rollout by 2026.

articles.title: The economic logic of sin taxes: Why they are a form of fiscal morality

The discussion highlights the intersection of fiscal policy and public health, though it does not reference specific Indian regulations or actions.
articles.raw_summary: What is often overlooked in ‘sin’ taxation is that it is fundamentally meant to address the social costs imposed on society by the consumption of harmful products. It therefore stands to reason that the revenue generated from such taxation should be used to mitigate these harms, notes former CBIC chairman Najib Shah.

articles.title: De-dollarisation, war, and debt: Why gold is regaining monetary relevance
articles.summary: Gold is gaining importance globally due to rising geopolitical tensions, high debt levels, and efforts to reduce reliance on the US dollar. Central banks, including those in emerging economies, are increasing gold purchases, reflecting concerns over fiat currency stability. This shift could impact India's foreign exchange reserves, trade policies, and monetary strategies as the global financial system moves toward a multipolar order.
articles.raw_summary: Gold is regaining prominence as global markets face geopolitical tensions, rising debt and de-dollarisation trends. Central bank buying, structural shifts in the financial system and weakening confidence in fiat currencies are driving demand. Despite short-term volatility, gold’s long-term outlook remains strong amid a transition towards a multipolar global economic order.

articles.title: Global leaders work to ease oil price surge as Trump signals more weeks of Iran war
articles.summary: Global leaders in New York and London are attempting to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route, amid ongoing military strikes between the U.S., Israel, and Iran. President Trump has indicated the conflict could persist for two to three more weeks. The surge in oil prices and regional instability may impact India's energy imports and economic policies, given its heavy reliance on crude oil from the Middle East.
articles.raw_summary: In New York and London, European and Middle Eastern countries tried to develop a plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which has largely been closed since the war in Iran began. Following President Trump's address predicting two to three more weeks of war, the U.S. and Israel continued to bomb Iran, and Iran continued its strikes on Gulf countries and Israel. Nick Schifrin reports.

articles.title: HAL imposes ‘contractual penalties’ on GE for Tejas engine delivery delay; PSU chief says Russia made presentation on Su-57 jet
articles.summary: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), a state-owned aerospace company, is imposing contractual penalties on General Electric (GE) for delays in delivering F404 engines for India's Tejas Mark 1A fighter jets. HAL expects to receive 20 engines by December and one more by April-end. Meanwhile, all 34 Tejas jets will resume flying from April 8 after software issues were resolved. The PSU chief also mentioned a Russian presentation on the Su-57 jet, highlighting ongoing defence engagements.
articles.raw_summary: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited anticipates receiving 20 F404 engines from General Electric by December, with a sixth for the Tejas Mark 1A expected by April-end. HAL is imposing contractual penalties on GE for engine supply delays. Meanwhile, all 34 Tejas jets are set to resume flying from April 8 after software issues were resolved, following a recent grounding incident.

articles.title: No public injury, no case: HC junks PIL against EC transfers of govt officials in poll-bound Bengal

The court found no public injury justifying judicial intervention in the EC's administrative decisions.

articles.title: In Nalanda, faith turned fatal
articles.summary: On March 31, a stampede at the Sheetla Mata temple in Nalanda, Bihar, killed nine people after over 10,000 devotees, mostly women, overwhelmed the facility. Victims' families allege priests allowed entry through the exit gate after collecting money, while authorities cite poor crowd management and inadequate arrangements. The tragedy raises concerns about safety protocols at religious sites and potential regulatory oversight failures.
articles.raw_summary: On March 31, over 10,000 devotees, mostly women, thronged the Sheetla Mata temple in Nalanda, Bihar. The temple, which normally holds 500-1,000 people, was overwhelmed, triggering a stampede that claimed nine lives. Victims’ families allege that priests let devotees enter through the exit gate after collecting money. Amit Bhelari reports on how poor crowd management and inadequate arrangements worsened the tragedy

articles.title: The architecture of resilience: Why India’s private credit stands apart
articles.summary: India’s private credit market stands resilient amid global stress due to its conservative fund structures, strict leverage norms, and closed-ended AIF framework. articles.title: US imposes 100% tariff on patented pharma imports: How it impacts India
articles.summary: The US has imposed up to 100% tariffs on patented drugs and certain metals, aiming to boost domestic manufacturing. While India's generic drug exports are largely exempt, firms producing branded medicines or inputs for patented drugs may face pressure. Developed nations are expected to bear the brunt of these new measures.

While India's generic drug exports are largely exempt, firms producing branded medicines or inputs for patented drugs may face pressure. Developed nations are expected to bear the brunt of these new measures.

articles.title: EC hands over Malda violence probe involving judicial officers in Bengal's Malda to NIA
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India has handed over the investigation into violence in Malda, West Bengal, involving judicial officers, to the National Investigation Agency (NIA). This follows a Supreme Court order directing the probe. The incident occurred on a Wednesday, and the EC referred to the apex court's directive in its April 2 letter to the NIA.
articles.raw_summary: In an April 2 letter to the National Investigating Agency, the poll body referred to the apex court's order and directed it to probe the Wednesday incident.

articles.title: Karnataka governor seeks review of move to scrap 3rd language marks in Class 10
articles.summary: Karnataka Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot has sought a review of the state government's decision to remove third-language marks from Class 10 exams. The move comes after 146,000 out of 164,000 students failed the third-language paper in the 2024-25 SSLC exams. The Governor's intervention highlights concerns over the policy's impact on student performance and may prompt further government action.
articles.raw_summary: Of the 164,000 students who couldn’t clear the Karnataka SSLC exams in 2024-25, a vast majority, 146,000, failed in the third-language paper.

articles.title: US Allies Work On Plan B For Hormuz Strait If Trump Walks Away
articles.summary: More than 40 US allies, including key partners, met to discuss contingency plans for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The move signals deep concern over the waterway's crisis, triggered by US-Iran tensions under President Trump's policies. The Strait is a critical global oil transit route, and any disruption could impact energy supplies. India, heavily reliant on oil imports, may face indirect consequences from such geopolitical developments.
articles.raw_summary: More than 40 US allies met Thursday to discuss plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, in a signal to President Donald Trump of the deep concern across the international community about the crisis in the waterway triggered by his war on Iran.

articles.title: LPG rumours: Centre urges remaining states to prevent panic buying after only 17 conduct briefings
articles.summary: The central government has urged states to intensify communication efforts to counter rumors about LPG supply shortages, which have led to panic buying. Only 17 states currently hold press briefings, and authorities are being asked to use regular press conferences and social media to reassure citizens about LPG availability. The goal is to prevent hoarding and black marketing of LPG cylinders.
articles.raw_summary: The Centre is urging states to boost communication efforts against LPG supply rumors, as inadequate public outreach fuels panic buying. Only 17 states currently hold press briefings. Authorities are asked to intensify proactive, regular communication via press conferences and social media to reassure citizens about LPG availability and distribution, while also curbing hoarding and black marketing.

articles.title: STT hike on futures and options takes effect from today — Here's all you need to know
articles.summary: The Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on equity futures and options has been increased by SEBI, effective immediately. The STT on equity options premium rose from 0.1% to 0.15%, on equity futures from 0.02% to 0.05%, and on equity options exercise from 0.125% to 0.15%. This change aims to adjust tax rates on derivative trading, impacting investors and market participants.
articles.raw_summary: STT on equity options premium has been raised to 0.15% from 0.1% earlier. The STT on equity futures has been raised to 0.05% from 0.02% earlier, while the STT on equity options exercise has been raised to 0.15% from 0.125% earlier.

articles.title: Energy shocks come and go, India’s strength runs deeper
articles.summary: The key takeaway is not that India is insulated from global shocks, but that it is better equipped to manage them, with stress concentrated rather than systemic, buffers meaningful but finite, and policy flexibility available but requiring judicious use, writes Bhavdeep Bhatt, CEO, Northern Arc Investment Managers.

articles.title: A decade and 61 cases later, Supreme Court grants divorce to end matrimonial dispute
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India resolved a decade-long matrimonial dispute by granting divorce to a couple and settling all claims between them. The court also quashed 61 related cases, bringing an end to the legal battle. This ruling highlights the judiciary's role in resolving long-standing disputes and ensuring legal closure for individuals.

articles.title: Control Over Strait of Hormuz Will Determine Who Wins the War
articles.summary: Iran is tightening its control over the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway for global oil shipments, by introducing new rules. The move aims to strengthen Iran's influence in the Middle East. This could disrupt oil supplies, affect energy prices, and impact India's crude oil imports, which rely heavily on the region. The situation may lead to policy discussions on energy security and diplomatic responses.
articles.raw_summary: Iran is seeking permanent leverage over the Middle East with new rules for the strategic waterway.

articles.title: UPI achieves nearly 23 billion transactions in March: DFS
articles.summary: The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) recorded 22.64 billion transactions in March, a 24% increase from the same period last year. The Department of Financial Services (DFS) highlighted this milestone as part of India's growing digital payment ecosystem. The surge underscores the expanding adoption of UPI, which is a key component of the government's push for a cashless economy and financial inclusion.
articles.raw_summary: Transaction through the Unified Payments Interface (UPI) touched a record high of 22.64 billion in volume terms in March. In volume terms, 22.64 billion transactions were carried during the month as compared to 18.3 billion a year ago, posting a growth of 24%.

articles.title: India’s tech hiring opens FY27 on a cautious note with fresher roles under most pressure: Report
articles.summary: Entry-level openings, i.e. roles targeting candidates with up to two years of experience, are flat at 15,000 for the second consecutive month, but are down nearly 11% year on year, giving jitters to freshers, the report said. Fresher-friendly roles account for barely 14% of total demand, with mid-senior professionals continuing to corner the lion's share at 54%.

articles.title: Manmade fibre exporters flag concerns arising from West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Rising crude oil prices, driven by the West Asia conflict, are increasing the cost of raw materials for India's manmade fibre industry. This is putting pressure on spinners and weavers, as customers are unable or unwilling to absorb the higher costs. The textile value chain is facing significant challenges due to these cost pressures.

articles.title: India faces second year of sugar deficit as mills close early
articles.summary: India's sugar output will fall short of demand for the second year. Lower cane yields are causing mills to close early. This will reduce domestic stockpiles and support local prices. Exports are also contributing to lower availability. The next season will begin with reduced opening stocks. This situation is expected to firm up sugar prices.

articles.title: India's sugar production rises 9% to 272.31 lakh tons till March 31: ISMA
articles.summary: India's sugar production saw a 9 percent rise in the 2025-26 season. However, fewer sugar mills are operational this year. articles.title: World food price rise set to continue if Iran war lasts, FAO says

The index remains nearly 20% below its 2022 peak following the Ukraine war. Analysts warn that prolonged conflict in Iran could further drive up food prices. The situation may prompt India to reassess its agricultural policies or trade strategies to mitigate food inflation.
articles.raw_summary: The FAO Food Price Index, which measures changes in a basket of globally traded food commodities, rose by 2.4% from its revised February level. It is 1% above its value a year ago, although nearly 20% below its March 2022 peak, reached after the start of the war in Ukraine.

These movements reflect market sentiment and could impact India's energy costs and inflation, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.

articles.title: Pakistan FM Dar discusses 5-point peace plan to end West Asia conflict with Saudi counterpart
articles.summary: Pakistan's Foreign Minister Dar met Saudi Arabia's Prince Faisal to propose a five-point peace plan aimed at resolving ongoing conflicts in West Asia. The initiative involves diplomatic discussions between the two countries. This development is significant for regional stability, which directly impacts India's foreign policy and security considerations in the Middle East.
articles.raw_summary: Pakistan's Foreign Minister Dar and Saudi counterpart Prince Faisal discussed a five-point peace initiative aimed at resolving ongoing conflicts in West Asia.

articles.title: Pakistan raises diesel rates by 54%, hikes petrol to nearly PKR 460 a litre amid fuel crisis
articles.summary: Pakistan has increased diesel prices by 54.9% to PKR 520.35 per litre and petrol prices by 42.7% to PKR 458.40 per litre amid a worsening fuel crisis. The move reflects severe economic pressures and could impact regional fuel markets, including potential implications for India's energy security and trade relations with Pakistan. The price adjustments are among the steepest in recent years, highlighting the country's economic challenges.
articles.raw_summary: The price of diesel in Pakistan would be raised by 54.9% to 520.35 PKR per litre, and petrol by 42.7% to 458.40 PKR per litre.
